title: BERT_CLASSIFIER block content
description: Maps a fine-tuned BERT model artefact onto a taxonomy slice. The classifier router resolves this block to pick the model that runs at the BERT tier of the cascade. Per architecture §8.2 and CSV #2 (Python trains, JVM serves).

modelVersion.description: Semantic version of the ONNX artefact (e.g. `2026.04.0`). The bert-inference service maps this to a MinIO object key under the configured artefacts bucket.

artifactRef.description: Optional explicit MinIO pointer. When absent, bert-inference resolves `modelVersion` against its configured artefacts bucket / prefix.

labelMapping.description: Maps the model's training labels onto taxonomy categories. Order is the model's softmax ordering — index 0 is the model's first class. The router consumes the matched `categoryId` plus the cascade-tier confidence.

modelLabel.description: Label as produced by the trainer (e.g. `hr_letter`, `finance_invoice`).

categoryId.description: Taxonomy category id this label maps to.

categoryName.description: Optional human-readable category name; the admin UI surfaces this without joining to the taxonomy collection.

trainingMetadata.description: Audit-friendly metadata about the artefact's provenance. Populated by the trainer; the inference service treats this as opaque.

evaluationMetrics.description: Held-out evaluation metrics — used by the admin UI's model-promotion screen.

minTextLength.description: Skip this tier if the input text is shorter than this. Useful when a model is unreliable on tiny inputs — fall through to SLM/LLM.
